channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former FBI Official Warns That Virus Response and CDC Staffing Cuts Pose Major Health Risks
item.description: A former high-ranking FBI official, Frank Figliuzzi, has raised concerns about significant vulnerabilities in the U.S. public health system due to actions taken during the Trump administration. Accord...

item.title: The Artistic Legacy of Petrus van Schendel: Master of Candlelit Scenes
item.description: Petrus van Schendel (1806–1870) was a prominent Dutch-Belgian painter of the Romantic era, celebrated for his exceptional skill in depicting night scenes illuminated by candles and moonlight. Often ca...

item.title: Majiha loses WBC title for not defending it
item.description: In a surprising move, the World Boxing Council (WBC) has officially stripped boxer Majiha of his championship title due to his failure to defend it within the scheduled timeframe. The decision comes a...

item.title: Evidence of Preserved Collagen Found in 66-Million-Year-Old Edmontosaurus Fossils
item.description: A groundbreaking study from the University of Liverpool has revealed that dinosaur fossils may still contain traces of original proteins, challenging the long-held assumption that fossilization comple...

item.title: Ontario Government Announces Expansion of Jail Capacity by 2,500 Beds
item.description: The Ontario government has unveiled a $3 billion plan to increase provincial jail capacity by approximately 2,500 beds over the next decade. Solicitor General Michael Kerzner announced the strategy, w...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange Reports Market Correction Amid Select Gains in Week 10 of 2026
item.description: During Week 10 of 2026, the Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) showed mixed performance. While some counters experienced strong gains, the overall market faced a correction. DCB led the weekly winners...

item.title: Daphne Opens Up to Katherine About Her History with Edward in Episode 42
item.description: In the latest episode of the popular ABS-CBN drama series 'The Alibi,' viewers witness an important turning point in the story. Daphne finally decides to share the truth about her past relationship wi...

item.title: Police arrest Congolese protesters during Africa-France summit in Nairobi
item.description: Police in Nairobi arrested nearly 100 Congolese nationals during demonstrations linked to the Africa-France Summit. The protesters were reportedly trying to stage actions near hotels hosting summit ac...

item.title: Pietermaritzburg High Court orders continuation of Zuma and Thales arms deal trial
item.description: The Pietermaritzburg High Court has ruled that the long-delayed corruption trial involving former president Jacob Zuma and the French arms company Thales will proceed. Judge Nkosinathi Chili emphasise...

item.title: 29th Cine Europa Film Festival Opens on May 28
item.description: The 29th edition of Cine Europa is set to open on May 28, 2026, bringing a diverse lineup of European films to audiences in the Philippines. Cine Europa, recognized for its dedication to showcasing in...

item.title: R&B Legend Clarence Carter Passes Away at 90
item.description: Clarence Carter, the renowned blues and soul singer-songwriter known for his distinctive raspy voice, has died at the age of 90. Born blind in Montgomery, Alabama, Carter taught himself guitar and pur...

item.title: Owen Sound Attack Hockey Team Sold but Remains in the City
item.description: The Owen Sound Attack, the local Ontario hockey team, has officially been sold to new ownership but will continue to operate in Owen Sound, ensuring that the community retains its familiar team. The s...

item.title: Three Arrested for Diverting Sh5.2 Million from Public Trustee Estates Account
item.description: Nairobi detectives have netted three suspects in connection to a well-planned fraud that saw Sh5,226,735 siphoned from the Public Trustee Administration Estates Account under the Office of the Attorne...

item.title: Analysis of the Indus Waters Treaty: India's Concessions and Pakistan's Advantages
item.description: The Indus Waters Treaty, signed on 19 September 1960 between India and Pakistan under the facilitation of the World Bank, established water-sharing arrangements for the Indus River System, which inclu...

item.title: Potential Labour Leadership Candidates Amid Starmer’s Unpopularity
item.description: Speculation is growing within Westminster that Keir Starmer’s position as Prime Minister may soon be under threat due to his low popularity with the UK public. Labour MPs have witnessed significant vo...

item.title: Pasig travel agency closed by DMW for alleged illegal recruitment of OFWs for US caregiver roles
item.description: The Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) recently ordered the closure of a travel agency in Pasig City due to alleged illegal recruitment activities targeting Filipinos for caregiver jobs in the United...

item.title: President Ramaphosa dismisses Social Development Minister Sisisi Tolashe amid misconduct allegations
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has officially dismissed Sisisi Tolashe from her position as Social Development Minister following growing allegations of corruption, misconduct, and misuse of public funds. ...

item.title: Dar es Salaam to Host 2026 ICGLR Women Parliamentarians Conference
item.description: Tanzania inayoenda kuwa mwenyeji wa mkutano wa wanawake wabunge kutoka nchi zote 12 za Jumuiya ya Mikoa ya Ziwa Kubwa (ICGLR) mwaka huu wa 2026. Takriban wabunge wanawake 200 watakusanyika Dar es Sala...

item.title: Trump Administration Reviews Green Cards of Certain Legal Permanent Residents
item.description: The Trump administration has initiated a new effort targeting legal permanent residents in the United States, according to a report by The New York Times. The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has...

item.title: Greenhushing in New Zealand: Challenges and Implications for Climate Risk Reporting
item.description: In New Zealand, climate reporting laws aimed at enhancing transparency are having an unintended effect: promoting 'greenhushing', where companies deliberately limit what they disclose about their clim...
